card_description: Avicenna is a divine or mythological figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick refines his theory of "orthogonal intervention," arguing that true change enters linear time "at right angles" from another dimension."

linked_segments.summary: Dick refines his theory of "orthogonal intervention," arguing that true change enters linear time "at right angles" from another dimension. He views his own "anamnesis" (memory restoration) as the key
